config.title: Spree River Berlin FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about the river that shaped Berlin, where to see it and whether a boat tour is worth it.

items.q: Why is the Spree River important to Berlin?
items.a: The Spree shaped the original settlement, trade routes, bridge crossings, Spree Island, Museum Island and the symbolic centre around the palace and Cathedral.

items.q: Where is the best place to see the Spree in central Berlin?
items.a: For one strong view, choose **Liebknecht Bridge**. It gives you the Spree, Berliner Dom, Humboldt Forum, Museum Island and often the TV Tower in one frame.

items.q: Is Museum Island actually an island?
items.a: Yes. Museum Island sits between branches of the Spree and its channels, which is why the museums feel separated from the rest of the historic centre.

items.q: Was the Spree part of the Berlin Wall border?
items.a: In some sections of divided Berlin, the Spree formed part of the border landscape, with banks, restricted zones and patrol areas turning the river into part of the Cold War divide.

items.q: Is a Spree boat tour worth it?
items.a: Yes, if you want a relaxed second perspective after walking. A boat tour is good for views of bridges, Museum Island and the government quarter, but it should not replace walking the historic centre.

items.q: Can I follow the Spree on foot?
items.a: Yes. A simple route runs from Alexanderplatz through Nikolaiviertel to Liebknecht Bridge, Museum Island, Friedrichsbruecke and Hackescher Markt.
